sections.paragraph:
πλοκή, ῆς, ἡ (πλέκω; Eur., Pla. et al.; pap; Ex 28:14; TestSol 21, 3 Q; EpArist) the condition of being braided, braiding, braid ὁ ἔξωθεν ἐκ πλοκῆς (ἐκπλοκῆς?) τριχῶν κόσμος 1 Pt 3:3 v.l. (for ἔξωθεν ἐμπλοκῆς τ. κ.).—DELG s.v. πλέκω.

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Q Q = Qumran, cited with numbers indicating cave of origin—List 2, end

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 831
